name: Ojos Negros Greenway and Regajo Reservoir Loop
name_original: Tour desde Segorbe a Jérica — Circular por el Embalse del Regajo y el Salto de la Novia
description: This moderate 20.7-mile mountain biking loop explores the Ojos Negros Greenway and offers scenic views of the Regajo Reservoir.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Segorbe offers diverse terrain, situated between the Sierra Calderona Natural Park to the south and the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park to the north. The region features extensive wooded areas of Aleppo pine and cork oak, alongside unique sandstone outcrops and deep ravines. Cyclists can also explore sections of the Ojos Negros Greenway, Spain's longest greenway, which provides varied surfaces from asphalt to compacted gravel. This landscape provides a range of routes from smooth paths to more rugged, challenging sections. Best mountain bike trails around Segorbe The most popular mountain bike trail is Ojos Negros Greenway and Regajo Reservoir Loop, a 20.7 miles (33.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 28 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows part of Spain's longest greenway and circles a scenic reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Salto de la Novia Waterfall – Pass Under the Rock loop from Segorbe - Arrabal, a difficult 28.6 miles (46.0 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and varied terrain, including a waterfall. Local mountain bikers also love the View of the Regajo Reservoir – View of the Regajo Reservoir loop from Segorbe - Ciudad, a 18.7 miles (30.1 km) trail leading through the local landscape, often completed in about 1 hour 56 minutes. Mountain biking around Segorbe is defined by the Sierra Calderona Natural Park, Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, and the Ojos Negros Greenway. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy reservoir loops to challenging mountain climbs. The routes in Segorbe are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 59 reviews. More than 748 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Segorbe's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Segorbe?
A: The region around Segorbe offers a diverse range of terrain for mountain biking. You'll find extensive wooded areas of Aleppo pine and cork oak in the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Calderona Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, offering smoother paths. To the north, the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Espadán Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; presents a more rugged landscape with unique sandstone outcrops, deep ravines, and more demanding sections. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931205536/ojos-negros-greenway-and-regajo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos Negros Greenway&lt;/a&gt; provides varied surfaces from asphalt to compacted gravel, often with gentle gradients.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Segorbe?
A: Yes, Segorbe offers options for all skill levels. Beginners and families can enjoy easier routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31975298" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Regajo Reservoir – View of the Regajo Reservoir loop from Segorbe - Ciudad&lt;/a&gt;, which is 18.7 miles (30.1 km) long and leads through local landscapes. The Ojos Negros Greenway, which passes through Segorbe, is also known for its generally gentle gradients and safe, family-friendly environment.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders near Segorbe?
A: Experienced riders looking for a challenge can explore routes with significant elevation gain and varied terrain. A notable difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31934132" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de la Novia Waterfall – Pass Under the Rock loop from Segorbe - Arrabal&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 28.6 miles (46.0 km) and features over 1,100 meters of ascent. The Sierra de Espadán Natural Park also offers more rugged and demanding trails with gradients up to 10% in some sections.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Segorbe?
A: Segorbe benefits from a mild Mediterranean climate, making it suitable for cycling year-round. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for longer rides, avoiding the peak heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that include scenic viewpoints or natural attractions?
A: Absolutely. Many routes around Segorbe incorporate scenic spots. For instance, you can find trails that pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1756231" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Regajo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1054060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garbí Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering spectacular views of the Gulf of Valencia, which can be a rewarding stop on a ride.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that pass through historic towns or villages?
A: Yes, some routes connect Segorbe with other charming towns. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19521523" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic center of Jérica – View of the Regajo Reservoir loop from Segorbe - Arrabal&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 32.6-mile (52.5 km) route that allows you to explore the historic center of Jérica while enjoying the surrounding landscape.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails in Segorbe typically take to complete?
A: The duration of mountain bike trails in Segorbe varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. For example, an easy route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31975298" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Regajo Reservoir loop&lt;/a&gt; (18.7 miles / 30.1 km) can be completed in under 2 hours, while a more challenging 28.6-mile (46.0 km) route such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31934132" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de la Novia Waterfall loop&lt;/a&gt; might take over 4.5 hours.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Segorbe?
A: The mountain bike trails around Segorbe are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 59 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from the quiet woodlands of Sierra Calderona to the rugged challenges of Sierra de Espadán, and the well-maintained sections of the Ojos Negros Greenway.

Q: Are there any routes that offer a mix of natural beauty and cultural sights?
A: Many routes in the Segorbe area provide a blend of natural beauty and cultural interest. The region's historical paths, originally used to connect villages or access mines, now offer mountain bikers a chance to experience both the natural landscapes and the rich history of the area. The proximity to towns like Segorbe and Jérica also means you can easily combine your ride with exploring local heritage.

Q: How many mountain bike routes are available in the Segorbe area?
A: There are over 70 mountain bike routes available around Segorbe, catering to various skill levels. This includes 6 easy routes, 40 moderate routes, and 27 difficult routes, ensuring a wide selection for every rider.

Q: Are there any specific natural parks that mountain bikers should explore near Segorbe?
A: Yes, Segorbe is uniquely positioned between two significant natural parks. To the south, the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Calderona Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; offers extensive wooded areas and smoother paths. To the north, the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Espadán Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, the second-largest protected area in the Valencian Community, provides a more rugged and challenging landscape with unique sandstone outcrops and lush cork oak forests.

Q: Can I find routes that are loops, starting and ending in the same place?
A: Many of the mountain bike routes around Segorbe are designed as loops, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931205536/ojos-negros-greenway-and-regajo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos Negros Greenway and Regajo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40378855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic center of Jérica – View of the Regajo Reservoir loop from Segorbe - Ciudad&lt;/a&gt;, allowing for circular journeys.
tips: The Regajo Reservoir is located between Navajas and Jucar. It's a beautiful green nature reserve that also offers recreational opportunities. The reservoir was built in 1959 to address the water shortage for irrigation. This water is intended for the orchards of the Sagunto region.

This charming village with over 1,600 inhabitants has a very charming center, beautiful historic buildings, and charming steep alleys.
Jerica is located in the Valencian Community in the province of Castellón at an altitude of 523 meters.
Colorful construction of an old mill in Navajas next to the Palancia River. From here it connects with the path of the sources and the greenway of Ojos Negros.
Beautiful and popular trail along several highlights

This beautiful 30m waterfall owes its name to its similarity to a bride's veil, although history hides a tragic legend.
They say in the town that when a couple wanted to get married, they had to go to the area where the river narrowed and once there, the girl would jump from one bank to the other. Before the eyes of the public, if everything went well, it was believed that the marriage would be prosperous, otherwise, a life full of unhappiness was predicted.
The tragedy came one day when a couple wanted to comply with the tradition but the young woman fell into the river that caught her in a whirlpool. Given the situation, her boyfriend jumped to save her but they both drowned. After this tragedy, the tradition was questioned until it stopped being practiced.
Nice path but after a few kilometers it is no longer passable, too many exposed roots, loose stones and rocks.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Ojos Negros Greenway and Regajo Reservoir Loop?
faqs.answer: The Ojos Negros Greenway, which this loop incorporates, passes through varied landscapes. While specific seasonal advice for this exact loop isn't provided, generally, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for mountain biking in this region, avoiding the intense heat of summer or potential chill of winter. Consider checking local weather forecasts before your ride.

faqs.question: Is the Ojos Negros Greenway and Regajo Reservoir Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This specific loop is rated as 'moderate' for mountain biking. While the broader Ojos Negros Greenway is generally considered easy to intermediate with gentle gradients, some sections around the Regajo Reservoir can be more challenging, requiring good fitness. It features a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces. If you're a beginner, be prepared for some ascents and potentially rougher terrain.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The tour starts near Segorbe. While specific parking details for this exact starting point aren't provided, you'll typically find parking available in or near towns that serve as access points to the Ojos Negros Greenway and Regajo Reservoir area. Look for designated parking areas in Segorbe or nearby villages like Jérica.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dog-friendliness for this specific loop is not explicitly stated. However, many sections of the Ojos Negros Greenway are generally accessible for dogs, often requiring them to be on a leash. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them. Check local signage for any specific restrictions in sensitive areas or nature reserves.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a diverse terrain, typical of the Ojos Negros Greenway. You'll encounter a mix of paved sections (like asphalt) and unpaved surfaces, including compacted gravel and earth. While much of the Greenway is known for its gentle gradients, this loop includes ascents and descents, contributing to its 'moderate' difficulty rating. Be prepared for varying surfaces under your tires.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Ojos Negros Greenway and Regajo Reservoir Loop?
faqs.answer: This loop offers several notable sights. You'll get a fantastic View of the Regajo Reservoir and its imposing dam. The route also passes through the Historic center of Jérica, and near the El Nogueral Mill. You'll also encounter sections of the Senda de las Fuentes (Navajas–Rio Palancia) and the scenic Salto de la Novia Trail, which leads to the beautiful Bride's Leap Waterfall.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to access the Ojos Negros Greenway or the Regajo Reservoir area for mountain biking. It's a public greenway and natural area. However, always be respectful of the environment and any private property you may pass near.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike loop connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route utilizes sections of several other trails. Notably, it covers about 32% of both the 'La Conquista de Valencia' and the 'Vía Verde Ojos Negros'. You'll also ride parts of the 'Camino del Cid (La Conquista de Valencia E08)' and the 'Nacimiento del Palancia', among others.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: The scenery is quite diverse and spectacular. You'll ride through areas with mountains, valleys, and forests, including pine and oak trees, as well as groves of oranges and olives. The route offers beautiful views of the Regajo Reservoir and its dam. As part of the Ojos Negros Greenway, you may also encounter historical elements like old railway structures, viaducts, and tunnels.

faqs.question: What is the duration of the Ojos Negros Greenway and Regajo Reservoir Loop?
faqs.answer: This mountain bike loop is approximately 33.3 kilometers long and typically takes around 2 hours and 28 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your fitness level, speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the views or explore points of interest.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or insider tips for this loop?
faqs.answer: While the Ojos Negros Greenway is generally accessible, this specific loop around the Regajo Reservoir is rated 'moderate' and includes some ascents. Be aware that some sections might be unpaved and more challenging, potentially requiring good fitness. If you're looking for a less strenuous ride, consider sticking to the flatter, paved sections of the main Greenway. However, the views of the reservoir are well worth the effort on this loop.
